model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current model is likely too capacity-limited for the 256-step budget. A simple width increase in both the convolutional trunk and classifier is a low-complexity way to reduce underfitting without changing the training contract or loop behavior.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","old":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The width increase helped, which suggests the model is still underpowered. Adding one more residual block is a compact way to raise representational capacity while preserving the current optimizer, schedule, and tensor shapes.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed 256-step budget, the model is still seeing too little of CIFAR-10. Doubling batch size is a simple way to expose it to substantially more examples without changing the optimizer or architecture that are already working better.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After increasing batch size, the optimization setup can likely support a somewhat larger step size. A moderate learning-rate increase is a simple way to exploit the larger effective data throughput without disturbing the architecture or scheduler.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The model is now learning meaningfully, so a light regularization change is worth testing. Label smoothing is a compact way to improve generalization and often lowers validation cross-entropy without needing architectural or optimizer changes.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Label smoothing slightly worsened validation loss, so the clean next move is to remove that regularization and return to the stronger plain cross-entropy baseline before testing other directions.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The recent gains still point to a capacity-limited regime. A moderate width increase is a straightforward next test that keeps the same training dynamics while giving the residual trunk and classifier more room to fit within the fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The larger model responded well to the current setup, and its increased capacity may support a slightly more aggressive step size. A modest learning-rate increase is the simplest next test to improve optimization within the same 256-step horizon.","secondary_modes":["layout","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels * CHANNEL_MULT, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels * CHANNEL_MULT) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","old":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"            nn.Linear(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, FC_HIDDEN),","old":"            nn.Linear(channels, FC_HIDDEN),","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current network is still spatially shallow. Introducing a simple downsampling transition into a wider second stage uses the existing channel multiplier, adds a more ResNet-like hierarchy, and may improve feature quality more than further flat width increases.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels * CHANNEL_MULT, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels * CHANNEL_MULT) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","old":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels * CHANNEL_MULT, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels * CHANNEL_MULT) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The staged architecture helped materially, so the next low-friction capacity increase is to deepen the higher-channel stage rather than widening everything again. That concentrates extra compute where the representation is richest and should fit the current optimization setup well.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The deeper staged model is still improving under the current schedule, so a small additional learning-rate increase is a cheap way to test whether optimization is still step-size limited within 256 updates.","secondary_modes":["layout","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The higher learning rate overshot and increased validation loss, so the clean next step is to revert to the better-performing 2e-3 setting before exploring other changes.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 3","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The staged architecture is working well, and the easiest targeted capacity increase now is to widen only the second stage. Raising the channel multiplier increases representational power where the model already benefits from deeper processing, without changing the training loop or optimizer.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-3","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The architecture has grown substantially and runtime is already high, so the next sensible move is a low-cost regularization adjustment rather than more capacity. A modest increase in weight decay can improve generalization without changing the model shape or training budget.","secondary_modes":["indexing","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The convolutional trunk is now strong, and the cheapest remaining capacity increase is in the classifier head. Widening the hidden layer should add flexibility with negligible extra training cost compared with altering the convolutional stages further.","secondary_modes":["caching","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.05","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The model now has substantially more capacity in both the deeper second stage and the classifier head. A very light dropout rate is a low-cost regularization test that may improve validation loss without materially affecting runtime or requiring further architectural changes.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.05","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Even a small dropout rate hurt validation loss noticeably, so the correct next move is to remove it and restore the stronger no-dropout baseline before using the remaining steps on other ideas.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Warmup improved the current setup, which suggests the optimizer may now tolerate a slightly stronger peak step size. A small learning-rate increase is the highest-upside final tweak that preserves the current architecture and schedule shape.","secondary_modes":["summaries","layout"]}
